name: Tableau MCP Server (Official)
what_it_does: Official Tableau MCP server enabling AI agents to interact with Tableau's business intelligence platform — querying published data sources and workbooks, executing VizQL queries, retrieving metadata, managing Tableau Server or Cloud content, and integrating Tableau's analytics layer into agent-driven data workflows.
use_cases: Querying Tableau published data sources from analytics agents
Retrieving dashboard and workbook metadata from content management agents
Running VizQL queries against Tableau data from data extraction agents
Managing Tableau Server content and permissions from admin agents
Embedding Tableau insights into agent-generated reports
Monitoring dashboard usage and performance from ops agents
not_for: Teams using Power BI, Looker, or Metabase as primary BI platform
Raw data warehousing (Tableau is a BI layer, not storage)
Teams without Tableau Server or Tableau Cloud subscription
best_when: An agent needs to query Tableau published analytics or manage Tableau Server/Cloud content for BI automation.
avoid_when: You're using Power BI, Looker, or another BI platform — or if you need direct database access rather than BI layer queries.

auth.notes: Tableau personal access tokens (PAT) for API authentication. Username/password also supported. PAT associated with specific Tableau site. Connected Apps for OAuth flows.

pricing.notes: Enterprise BI platform with per-role pricing. Tableau Server is self-managed (additional cost). Tableau Cloud is SaaS. MCP server is open source official.

performance.rate_limits: REST API: rate limits by Tableau version and configuration

known_agent_gotchas: Site ID required for all API calls — Tableau Server may have multiple sites
Personal access tokens tied to specific site and user — must use correct scope
VizQL Server API is different from REST API — each has different auth and endpoints
Tableau Server on-prem vs Tableau Cloud have different base URLs and some feature differences
Content (workbooks, data sources) must be published before it's queryable via API
API version must match Tableau Server version — newer features require newer API versions
agent_readiness.security_notes: HTTPS enforced. Personal access tokens with site-level scope. Connected Apps for OAuth. SOC 2, ISO 27001, HIPAA, GDPR, FedRAMP. Official Tableau MCP server.

security_score_components.security_notes: HTTPS enforced. Personal access tokens with site scope. Connected Apps for OAuth. SOC 2, ISO 27001, HIPAA, GDPR, FedRAMP. Official Tableau MCP server.
